emails.subject: Cross Receipt
emails.body: Mr. Hayslett: Travis McCullough asked me to track down the Cross Receipt which was returned to him, possibly today. Do you recall what floor it was directed to? This probably sounds like a stupid question, but Travis has been working out of several offices here, on both 8 and 38, and he is in New York now. I just want to be sure we do not lose the Cross Receipt. Thank you. Mary Heinitz Senior Legal Specialist mary.j.heinitz@enron.com 713-853-3245 (phone) 713-646-3393 (fax)

emails.subject: Re: Cross Receipt
emails.body: Re: Cross Receipt Sent to 8 Sent from my BlackBerry Wireless Handheld (<URL>

emails.subject: RE: Cross Receipt
emails.body: RE: Cross Receipt Thank you. To: Heinitz, Mary J.

emails.subject: RE: Cross Receipt
emails.body: RE: Cross Receipt Sorry to bother you again, but was it sent in an interoffice mail envelope or hand-delivered? It has not turned up yet. To: Heinitz, Mary J.

emails.subject: RE: Cross Receipt
emails.body: RE: Cross Receipt Sorry for the earlier message. It just arrived and I am having it picked up. To: Heinitz, Mary J.

emails.subject: RE: Cross Receipt
emails.body: RE: Cross Receipt Itwas in a yellow interoffice envelope, real yellow, not the regular manila colored. Sent from my BlackBerry Wireless Handheld (<URL>
